quick_skim.title: Learning Latency-Aware Orchestration for Multi-Agent Systems
quick_skim.abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) coordinate multiple LLM-powered agents through structured workflows, gaining reasoning power but incurring high inference latency from multi-step execution and repeated model invocations. Existing orchestration methods primarily optimize task performance and inference cost, leaving latency largely unaddressed. In MAS, end-to-end latency is governed by the critical execution path, so reducing total cost alone does not reliably reduce latency. Moreover, optimizing latency while preserving accuracy remains non-trivial: naive latency optimization can misassign operator-level credit and degrade task accuracy. To address this gap, we propose Latency-Aware Multi-agent System (LAMaS), a latency-aware orchestration framework for learning-based multi-agent systems. LAMaS addresses this challenge at two levels: at training time, it learns latency-aware execution graphs through constrained optimization with critical-path-aware credit assignment; at inference time, since a graph committed at training time cannot exploit runtime evidence, it complements graph construction with a lightweight controller that adaptively eliminates redundant future agent interactions as execution unfolds. Experiments on four benchmarks show that LAMaS achieves the best latency among evaluated learning-based MAS baselines, reducing end-to-end latency by over 50\% while maintaining competitive or better accuracy. LAMaS is also modular and transfers to other MAS with minimal changes, consistently yielding latency reductions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: focuses on latency-aware orchestration in multi-agent systems with multiple LLM agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 关注多LLM智能体系统的延迟感知编排
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 关注多LLM智能体系统的延迟感知编排
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 关注多LLM智能体系统的延迟感知编排
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LAMaS, a latency-aware orchestration framework for multi-agent systems that optimizes end-to-end latency while preserving task accuracy using learning-based methods.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 多智能体系统推理延迟高，现有方法忽视端到端延迟优化。提出LAMaS框架，通过学习优化关键路径延迟，同时保持任务准确率。实验表明在多个MAS场景中显著降低延迟而不损害性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 多智能体系统推理延迟高，现有方法忽视端到端延迟优化。提出LAMaS框架，通过学习优化关键路径延迟，同时保持任务准确率。实验表明在多个MAS场景中显著降低延迟而不损害性能。

quick_skim.title: Communication-Efficient Digital-Twin Coordination for Heterogeneous LLM Embodied Agents over Computing Power Networks
quick_skim.abstract: Embodied agent teams powered by heterogeneous large language models (LLMs) are being widely deployed in physical artificial intelligence such as smart factories, warehouses, and service robotics. To enable collaboration among such an agent team, efficient coordination mechanisms that operate reliably under limited network resources are required. However, existing heterogeneous LLM-agent coordination frameworks that rely on multi-round natural-language-based conversations introduce three coupled challenges. First, inter-agent dialogue incurs communication overhead that grows rapidly with team size. Second, the quality of coordination is constrained by the heterogeneous capabilities of the agent team's LLMs. Third, agents may suffer from action delays due to iterative negotiation. To address these challenges, we propose LDT-Coord, a networked coordination framework built upon a lightweight digital twin (DT). Specifically, each agent independently selects its intended action and reports both the action decision and a structured temporal constraint over shared resources to the DT server, thereby decoupling coordination performance from natural-language reasoning ability. Then, DT executes a training-free, rule-based orchestrator algorithm to resolve cross-agent conflicts and returns coordination instructions to prevent such conflicts. To further reduce communication overhead, we formulate agent reporting control as a constrained partially observable Markov decision process (C-POMDP) and solve it with the PPO-Lagrangian algorithm. Simulation results show that LDT-Coord achieves a task success rate comparable to conventional coordination methods while reducing communication overhead by more than 70x and maintaining robustness under LLM heterogeneity.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体协调与通信优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体协调与通信优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体协调与通信优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a communication-efficient digital-twin coordination framework for heterogeneous LLM embodied agents, addressing overhead and capability heterogeneity in multi-agent teams.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对异构LLM具身智能体团队中多轮自然语言对话带来的通信开销大和协调质量受限于模型能力的问题，提出了一种基于数字孪生的通信高效协调框架。通过优化智能体间的信息交换机制，在有限网络资源下实现可靠协作。实验表明该方法能有效降低通信成本并保持协调性能，为多智能体系统在物理AI中的应用提供了实用方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对异构LLM具身智能体团队中多轮自然语言对话带来的通信开销大和协调质量受限于模型能力的问题，提出了一种基于数字孪生的通信高效协调框架。通过优化智能体间的信息交换机制，在有限网络资源下实现可靠协作。实验表明该方法能有效降低通信成本并保持协调性能，为多智能体系统在物理AI中的应用提供了实用方案。

quick_skim.title: COLMAR: Cooperative View Policy Learning for Multi-Agent Active 3D Reconstruction
quick_skim.abstract: Active 3D reconstruction requires selecting informative viewpoints under limited sensing budgets. In multi-agent settings, coordination inefficiencies such as redundant observations and spatial clustering can significantly reduce reconstruction quality. We present COLMAR, a cooperative view policy learning framework for multi-agent active 3D reconstruction. COLMAR formulates viewpoint allocation as a shared policy optimization over map-centric observations and introduces a reconstruction-aware objective that promotes overlap-aware coverage, team-level discovery, and collision-safe exploration. Dense feedback derived from incremental reconstruction updates aligns exploration behavior with downstream geometric quality. The policy is trained using parameter-sharing Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) with independent per-agent action selection at deployment, conditioned on a fused team map and without inter-agent message passing for decision making. Selected viewpoints are then reconstructed with 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) for high-fidelity photometric evaluation. Experiments on GLEAM and Replica demonstrate consistent improvements over heuristic and non-cooperative baselines, achieving up to 54% higher reconstruction accuracy and 49% greater coverage under matched sensing budgets.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: cooperative multi-agent view policy learning for 3D reconstruction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体协作视角策略学习用于3D重建
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体协作视角策略学习用于3D重建
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体协作视角策略学习用于3D重建
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes COLMAR for multi-agent active 3D reconstruction, using shared policy optimization with reconstruction-aware objectives to promote overlap-aware coverage and collision-safe exploration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出COLMAR多智能体协作视角策略学习框架，解决主动3D重建中的冗余观测和空间聚类问题。将视角分配建模为共享策略优化，引入重建感知目标以促进重叠感知覆盖和团队探索。实验验证了重建质量的提升，但未涉及注意力机制。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出COLMAR多智能体协作视角策略学习框架，解决主动3D重建中的冗余观测和空间聚类问题。将视角分配建模为共享策略优化，引入重建感知目标以促进重叠感知覆盖和团队探索。实验验证了重建质量的提升，但未涉及注意力机制。

quick_skim.title: Task-Oriented Sensing and Covert Transmissions for Collaborative Multi-AUV Systems
quick_skim.abstract: In underwater covert cooperative missions, autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) often cannot rely on active sonar to continuously obtain complete information, since active sensing and frequent communications increase the risk of exposure. As a result, AUVs primarily rely on passive observation, an approach that yields incomplete local perception and limited task efficiency. Although underwater acoustic communications can mitigate this limitation through information sharing, they are simultaneously constrained by long delays, severe interference, low reliability, and the risk of covert exposure. Existing communications-oriented multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) studies often model communication as an ideal information flow, whereas traditional communication optimization primarily focuses on link-level performance. However, both are insufficient to characterize the actual contribution of perceptual information to cooperative tasks under realistic conditions of covert physical communications. This paper proposes a Sensed Information Value Realization Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (SVR-MARL) framework that leverages practical information to characterize the utility of information for cooperative tasks and learns distributed cooperative policies under realistic communication and covert constraints. Through a case study of covert multi-AUV cooperative localization and tracking, the potential of the proposed framework to improve collaborative task efficiency while reducing unnecessary communication and exposure risks is demonstrated.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-AUV system with cooperative communication and covert transmissions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多自主水下机器人系统，涉及协作通信与隐蔽传输
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多自主水下机器人系统，涉及协作通信与隐蔽传输
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多自主水下机器人系统，涉及协作通信与隐蔽传输
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a task-oriented sensing and covert transmission framework for collaborative multi-AUV systems, optimizing communication efficiency and covertness in underwater missions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对水下多AUV协同任务中主动探测暴露风险与通信限制，提出一种面向任务的感知与隐蔽传输框架。该方法结合多智能体强化学习与通信优化，在保证隐蔽性的同时提升任务效率。仿真实验验证了框架的有效性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对水下多AUV协同任务中主动探测暴露风险与通信限制，提出一种面向任务的感知与隐蔽传输框架。该方法结合多智能体强化学习与通信优化，在保证隐蔽性的同时提升任务效率。仿真实验验证了框架的有效性。
